behavior_summary: This script manages an egg object's U8P_FISHEGG::hatch lifecycle. U8P_FISHEGG::hatch guards starting a U8P_FISHEGG::hatch sequence by checking the egg's stored id (Egg::getEggId(this)). If the id is 0 (uninitialized), U8P_FISHEGG::hatch sets the id to 1 (Egg::setEggId(1, this)) and launches a timed U8P_FISHEGG::hatch/animation routine (U8P_FISHEGG::func00CC).

U8P_FISHEGG::func00CC implements the U8P_FISHEGG::hatch animation and outcome: it creates a temporary item for visuals, places (pop) it at the egg's coordinates, advances visual frames in a loop with short timed waits (spawn ->FREE::func1C19 + suspend), plays SFX at specific frames, uses urandom to choose between two visual/outcome branches, and destroys the temporary item at the end.
U8P_FISHEGG::leaveFastArea resets the egg state by clearing its stored id (Egg::setEggId(0, this)), inferred to cancel or allow retriggering when the actor leaves the trigger area.
notable_control_flow: Guard: U8P_FISHEGG::hatch reads Egg::getEggId(this) and only proceeds if the id == 0; otherwise it does nothing.
State set + kickoff: U8P_FISHEGG::hatch sets Egg::setEggId(1, this) to mark initialization and calls U8P_FISHEGG::func00CC to run the sequence.

Timed animation loop: U8P_FISHEGG::func00CC creates a temporary item and runs a frame counter (local1) that is updated each iteration, using spawn/suspend to delay between frames, triggering playSFX at specific frames and finally destroying the temporary item.
Randomized branch: U8P_FISHEGG::func00CC uses urandom to pick between two branches which choose different temporary item types, starting frames, frame ranges, and SFX timing.
Reset on exit: U8P_FISHEGG::leaveFastArea clears the egg id to 0, allowing the U8P_FISHEGG::hatch guard to be satisfied again on re-entry or retrigger.
open_questions: What specific gameplay consequences correspond to nonzero egg id values beyond '1' (are other ids used elsewhere to represent hatched outcomes)?
Exact meanings of numeric constants (item type ids, frame indices, SFX ids and spawn/timer delay values) — the code uses literals whose gameplay mapping is not explicit here.
Whether U8P_FISHEGG::leaveFastArea is invoked by an engine-area-exit event (fast-area leave) or some other trigger in all contexts where the egg should reset.

main_function.name: U8P_FISHEGG::hatch
main_function.purpose: Guard and initiate an egg U8P_FISHEGG::hatch sequence: if the egg's stored id is 0, mark it initialized (set to 1) and start the timed U8P_FISHEGG::hatch/animation routine.
entry_conditions: Invoked as the egg's U8P_FISHEGG::hatch/trigger process (likely when the player/actor enters a fast-area or triggers the egg).
Only proceeds when Egg::getEggId(this) == 0 (egg in uninitialized state).

function_map.name: U8P_FISHEGG::hatch
function_map.role: Guard + kickoff for U8P_FISHEGG::hatch sequence; checks egg id, sets it to 1, calls the timed sequence.

function_map.name: U8P_FISHEGG::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Reset behavior: clears egg id (Egg::setEggId(0, this)) when the trigger/area is left to allow retriggering.

function_map.name: U8P_FISHEGG::func00CC
function_map.role: Timed U8P_FISHEGG::hatch/animation and outcome routine: create/pop temporary item, run frame loop with spawn/suspend, play SFX, randomize branch, destroy temp item.

internal_calls.purpose: Start the timed U8P_FISHEGG::hatch/animation/outcome routine after setting the egg id to indicate initialization.

external_calls.purpose: Read the egg's stored id/state to decide whether to start the U8P_FISHEGG::hatch sequence (guard).
Set the egg's stored id to 1 to mark initialization/start of U8P_FISHEGG::hatch sequence.
Clear/reset egg id to 0 when leaving the trigger area so the U8P_FISHEGG::hatch can be retriggered.
Generate a random roll to choose between two U8P_FISHEGG::hatch visual/outcome branches.
Create short timer processes used with suspend to implement per-frame delays in the animation loop.
Yield the U8P_FISHEGG::hatch process until the spawned timer completes to space animation frames.
Create the temporary item used for U8P_FISHEGG::hatch visuals (different item ids chosen per random branch).
Place the created temporary item into the world at the egg's coordinates (uses Item::getZ/Y/X on the egg).
Read egg's Z coordinate for placing the temporary item.
Read egg's Y coordinate for placing the temporary item.
Read egg's X coordinate for placing the temporary item.
Advance the temporary item's visual frame each loop iteration to animate U8P_FISHEGG::hatch progress.
Play sound effects at specific animation frames to accent the U8P_FISHEGG::hatch progression.
Destroy/remove the temporary item when the animation/sequence completes.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script manages an egg object's U8P_FISHEGG::hatch lifecycle. U8P_FISHEGG::hatch guards starting a U8P_FISHEGG::hatch sequence by checking the egg's stored id (Egg::getEggId(this)). If the id is 0 (uninitialized), U8P_FISHEGG::hatch sets the id to 1 (Egg::setEggId(1, this)) and launches a timed U8P_FISHEGG::hatch/animation routine (U8P_FISHEGG::func00CC).
